These constellations do not coincide with the division into
movable, fixed and common signs. These nakshatras partake
the natures of two signs. This again makes tabulation into the
triplicity of signs problematic.
Births can occur at the time of Sunrise also. Dr.
B.Satyanarayana Rao denies this in his book the Nadi Rectifi
cation. He has prepared the tables for the rectification of the
time of birth. He rightly remarks that for the purpose of
rectification we need the joint operation of(i) the lagna and its
triplicity, (ii) the Constellation at the time ofbirth, (iii) the
weekdays, and (iv) the time of Sunrise.
The nakshatras in the following tables are given by their
numerals, countings from Asvini to Revati as 1 to 27. The
lagnas rising at birth are given as movable, fixed, and com
mon. The timin&'s are given from the local time of sunrise,

72 Rectification of Birth Time
assuring it to be 6.00 A.M. If the local time of sunrise is before
or after 6.00 A.M.
